title: Dynabeads™ Cell Separation Protocol
author: Manuela De Las Casas
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.8sbhwan

steps.title: Thoroughly resuspend the Dynabeads™ magnetic beads...
steps.instruction: Thoroughly resuspend the Dynabeads™ magnetic beads in the vial using a pipette or a vortex. This must be done each time the beads are going to be used.

steps.title: Transfer 100 μL (4 mg) Dynabeads™ to a 1
steps.instruction: Transfer 100 μL (4 mg) Dynabeads™ to a 1.5 mL eppendorf tube. Place the tube in the magnetic rack for 2 min, to ensure separation. Then aspirate and discard the supernatant. Add your cell sample (Sample C o , prepared in 1X Binding/Wash Buffer) to the beads. Mix well.

steps.title: Incubate for 5 min at room...
steps.instruction: Incubate for 5 min at room temperature in a balance shaker (moving gently). The incubation time may be increased up to 10 min.

steps.title: Place the tube on the magnet...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on the magnet rack for 2 min, then remove the supernatant and keep it in a new tube (Sample C nb ).

steps.title: Wash the beads 3 times with...
steps.instruction: Wash the beads 3 times with 400 μL 1X Binding/Wash Buffer by placing the tube on the magnet for 2 min and remove the supernatant to a new tube. Resuspend the beads thoroughly between each washing step. The supernatant is kept in the same tube for every wash (Sample C w ).

steps.title: Add 100 μL His-Elution Buffer
steps.instruction: Add 100 μL His-Elution Buffer. Incubate the suspension for 5 min at room temperature in the balance shaker (moving gently).

steps.title: Apply on the magnet for 2...
steps.instruction: Apply on the magnet for 2 min and transfer the supernatant containing the eluted histidine-tagged cell to a clean tube (Sample C f ).

steps.title: Resuspend the remaining beads in 100...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the remaining beads in 100 μL LB and transfer it to a new tube (Sample C ne ). Proceed to cell counting:

steps.title: a
steps.instruction: a. Make serial dilutions of each of the samples until a concentration of 10 -4 in LB. Plate 100 μL of the each final dilution in LB agar + kanamicine plates. b. Count the cells by using a OD relationship.
